texto: It's near impossible to always be right when you're innovating. It's easy to get emotionally invested in it, but you need to monitor its impact like a hawk and react if you don't like what you see. And if/when you pull it back, you want to do that communication with transparency and humility.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jeff Jordan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “It's near impossible to always be right when you're innovating. It's easy to get emotionally inve…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, humility, impossible pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de impossible e easy.

frase_original: It's near impossible to always be right when you're innovating. It's easy to get emotionally invested in it, but you need to monitor its impact like a hawk and react if you don't like what you see. And if/when you pull it back, you want to do that communication with transparency and humility.
